mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.05 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in high PAYEMS periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 3.5% signals tight labor market conditions that pressure wage inflation, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or UNRATE decline correlates positively with returns (Pearson r > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in CPIAUCSL (inflation) and FEDFUNDS (interest rates) signal aggressive monetary tightening that raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 60-day returns in > 50% of interaction events, or individual series effects dominate over joint behavior (interaction IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS > 4.0%), rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals resilient supply-side dynamics that offset rate hikes, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or INDPRO shows no correlation with returns during high FEDFUNDS regimes (Pearson r < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Monthly growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding supply-side capacity that lowers inflationary pressures and supports equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or INDPRO growth correlates negatively with returns (Pearson r < -0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong demand for residential construction, boosting related sectors and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 60-day returns in > 55% of periods with HOUST growth, or HOUST fails to outperform baseline returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in real personal income (RSAFS) and inflation (CPIAUCSL) signal strong demand-side momentum that drives corporate revenues, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20-day returns in > 55% of interaction events, or individual series effects dominate over joint behavior (interaction IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: During demand expansion phases (GDPC1 growth > 2.0% QoQ), rising housing starts (HOUST) amplify sectoral demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction effect size falls below 0.05 (IC < 0.05).
